name: Kindergarten Reading — Semester 2
description: An 18-week, play-based Kindergarten pathway for decoding simple words, informational texts, and responding with evidence. Lessons are brief, hands-on, flexible, and designed for adult-guided learning.
learningGoals: Build confidence and curiosity with decoding simple words, informational texts, and responding with evidence.
Communicate learning through talk, drawing, movement, play, or a simple creation.
Practice safe, kind, and responsible participation.

lessons.objective: I can notice details and share a question about decoding simple words, informational texts, and responding with evidence through a close look at decoding simple words, informational texts, and responding with evidence.

units.title: Wonder and notice: decoding simple words, informational texts, and responding with evidence
units.essentialQuestion: What can we notice and wonder about decoding simple words, informational texts, and responding with evidence?
outcomes: I can notice details and share a question about decoding simple words, informational texts, and responding with evidence.
I can use words, pictures, movement, or materials to communicate my thinking.

activities.title: A close look at decoding simple words, informational texts, and responding with evidence
activities.learnerAction: With an adult or teacher nearby when needed, the learner participates in a close look at decoding simple words, informational texts, and responding with evidence, uses words, pictures, movement, or materials to communicate thinking, and cleans up safely.

successCriteria: The learner participates safely and respectfully in decoding simple words, informational texts, and responding with evidence.
The learner communicates one observation, choice, or question using speech, drawing, movement, or a supported response.
